chunk_summary: Anemia Mukt Bharat (AMB) integrates age-specific iron-folic acid prophylaxis and deworming into pediatric nutrition care, emphasizing documentation and longitudinal program action for anemia prevention and management.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-specific prophylaxis schedules for iron-folic acid supplementation under Anemia Mukt Bharat.
Learn the importance of integrating deworming rounds with anemia prevention programs.
Develop skills to document and monitor adherence, side effects, and dosing schedules for anemia prophylaxis.
Differentiate between prophylaxis and therapeutic management of anemia in children and adolescents.
key_facts: Children 6–59 months: Biweekly IFA syrup (1 mL containing 20 mg elemental iron and 100 mcg folic acid).
Children 5–10 years: Weekly IFA tablets (45 mg elemental iron and 400 mcg folic acid).
Adolescents 10–19 years: Weekly IFA tablets (60 mg elemental iron and 500 mcg folic acid).
Deworming rounds are integrated into anemia prevention and should be documented as part of the same package.
Prophylaxis should continue even during diagnostic evaluation for symptomatic anemia.
Documentation template includes last dose date, adherence pattern, side effects, and next dose location (AWC, school, VHSND).
reference_quote: Age-structured prophylaxis under national guidance is audit-ready. For children 6–59 months, the usual schedule is biweekly IFA syrup 1 mL (20 mg elemental iron plus 100 mcg folic acid) with delivery through community platforms.
document_key_topics: Iron-folic acid supplementation schedules for children and adolescents
Integration of deworming with anemia prevention programs
Documentation and monitoring practices for anemia prophylaxis
